pain_point_label: Contractor Coordination Overhead and Cost Overruns for Specialist Repairs

matches.match_rationale: The core of this pain point is a fragmented, undocumented contractor knowledge base — each campus holds its own informal relationships, pricing history, and performance impressions with no cross-campus visibility. Knowledge extraction can turn unstructured sources (past invoices, email threads, work orders, complaint logs) into a structured contractor graph: entity (contractor) → attributes (specialty, campus used, cost range) → relationships (who hired them, for what, outcome). This directly addresses the 'unknown contractor at another campus' and 'no performance history' mechanisms.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A one-time ingestion pipeline that processes historical invoices, email correspondence, and maintenance logs across all campuses to populate a shared contractor registry — with extracted entities for contractor name, trade, location, cost ranges, and performance flags — bootstrapping the master contractor list the org currently lacks.

matches.match_rationale: Contractor quotes, invoices, and payment milestones arrive in heterogeneous formats (PDFs, emails, spreadsheets) across many campuses with no shared schema. Data transformation AI can normalize these semi-structured inputs into a consistent format — contractor ID, job type, site, quoted cost, actual cost, timeline — enabling cross-campus cost comparison for the first time. This directly addresses the 'identical repairs vary significantly across campuses' symptom by making like-for-like comparison possible.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated intake layer that ingests incoming contractor quotes and invoices from all campuses, maps them to a standard schema, and feeds a shared dashboard showing cost variance by trade type and campus — giving procurement leadership immediate visibility into pricing inconsistencies.

matches.match_rationale: Once contractor cost data is normalized across campuses, anomaly detection can flag invoices or quotes that deviate significantly from expected cost ranges for a given trade type, job scope, or campus — surfacing overpricing, scope creep, or duplicate billings before payment is processed. This directly addresses the 'cost disputes' and 'hidden costs' pain mechanism by catching outliers systematically rather than relying on a manager's intuition.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A cost anomaly monitor that scores each incoming contractor invoice against historical baselines for that trade and job type across all campuses, flagging invoices above a threshold for human review before payment authorization — reducing cost overruns without requiring full procurement transformation.

matches.match_rationale: Multi-campus operators have no baseline for what a repair should cost, which is why contractors can charge inconsistent prices with no pushback. A regression model trained on historical job cost data (trade type, job scope, materials, campus location, urgency) can generate expected cost ranges for new quotes — giving campus managers a reference figure before accepting or negotiating. This directly addresses the 'no negotiating leverage' mechanism by giving managers an AI-derived benchmark.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A quote evaluation tool where a campus manager inputs the job type and basic scope, and the system returns an expected cost range derived from cross-campus historical data — enabling data-backed negotiation and early flagging of overpriced quotes before work is awarded.

matches.match_rationale: Campus managers currently have no way to query the group's collective contractor experience — who has been used before, at what cost, with what outcome. A RAG system over the contractor registry, past job records, and performance notes lets a campus manager ask 'who do we use for HVAC in the northern region?' and get a grounded answer with source citations. This directly addresses the 'redundant sourcing' and 'no master contractor list' mechanisms by making institutional knowledge queryable.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An internal contractor knowledge assistant where staff can query past contractor engagements across all campuses — getting ranked suggestions with historical pricing, quality notes, and contact details — reducing the time and risk of sourcing contractors from scratch for each job.

matches.match_rationale: The contractor engagement workflow — obtaining quotes, issuing work orders, tracking milestones, triggering payments, logging completion — is a multi-step process that currently runs manually and inconsistently across campuses with no centralized oversight. A task automation agent can orchestrate this workflow: receive a maintenance request, route it to pre-approved contractors, collect and compare quotes, issue award notifications, track milestone submissions, and flag delays or overruns. This addresses the root cause of inconsistency and manual overhead.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A contractor workflow automation layer that standardizes the end-to-end engagement process across all campuses — from job request to final payment — with automatic milestone tracking, SLA alerts, and cost variance flags, reducing site manager administrative burden while creating an auditable procurement trail.
